riders_group_posts.text: Axis Fireball 1750 Brand New, never used and purchased new directly from Axis. $1,420. Mukilteo, WA.

riders_group_posts.text: Anyone in Raglan happen to have a spare set of mast bolts and sliders? I'm in desperate need.
riders_group_posts.notable: Jon Verhoek (Top contributor, Hamilton) offered to short-term loan some. 2 more replies pending.

riders_group_posts.text: Which wings are easy to pump on SUP? I'm 175lbs and currently SUP foil, para wing (+dw), and dockstart on the 1300v1. I also have an Art Pro 1201 and a 999v2 but I cannot consistently pump either of them. I hear that the 1201 is supposed to be a pump machine, but as soon as I lose speed it drops out hard on me. I also can't flat water popup on it, but the 1300 is starting to feel real big in the surf and slow in the bumps. Is there a larger Surge coming?
riders_group_posts.notable: ASKING IF A LARGER SURGE IS COMING - product line intel

key_comments.text: Did you try the png1300v2? Of course one shouldn't use it in overhead conditions but it does handle some size and I find it quite nice in the turn. For me it works for dock start and DW (wing and PW) and light wind wing. It is not as fast as the 1201 but [truncated]
Nick what happened? You did over a minute on my set up? Was it the set up or the instructor?

riders_group_posts.text: Adrian Roper - new scootpump foil board looks sweet on the Fireball 1750. [linked foilingfreaks.com/scootpump]
riders_group_posts.notable: HIGH ENGAGEMENT 37 reactions, 15 comments. Scootpump = controversial device that goes viral

key_comments.text: friends don't let friends ride scooters!
Do you actually attach a board on top of that rail? Or go full minimalist?

riders_group_posts.text: Following on from the pumping question - here is my hot take after 10 days straight on the surge 1010. It will improve your pumping immensely. [See more truncated]
riders_group_posts.notable: Detailed Surge 1010 review/endorsement. Mark Shinn (All-star) commented with Surge vs Fireball comparison.

key_comments.text: Can I ask you how much you weigh.

I've been swapping back and forth between the Surge and the Fireball a lot the last days trying to decide where my heart lies... and for wing, parawing and Foil Drive action it is Surge all the way. The fireball is more efficient without a doubt and leaps forwards with every pump and size for size has a slightly better glide path BUT the Surge is so much more playful and forgiving of mistakes that it wins hands down for my riding style. The only exception is SUP foil downwind. Whilst I am a competent DW sup foiler, paddling up still gets my heart beating and I tend to go for distance rather than flowy carvy type dw runs. In this the Fireball shows its true colours (and dare I say it, justifies its place in the lineup!)

riders_group_posts.text: My new Surge 830 arrived, yeow [reel video]
80 kgs, looking to learn parawing on 90 litre midlength. Surge 1010 or Spitfire 1030/1100? I have the Surge 950, even if it gets up real easy I think it is on the small side for me to learn parawing?

key_comments.text: The spitfire is an awesome wing wing and has been for a few years now but the Surge has the benefit of several years more knowledge and R and D and is one of the new high camber foil designs. The early starting, pump and glide and of course playfulness... [see more]

1010 Surge is my light wind weapon for wingfoiling. I'm 85 kg. I can't comment on Parawing specifically, but if your board gets you going easily enough on your 950, then Surge 1010 could be the ideal wing. I had the Spitfire 1030 previously and like it... [see more]

riders_group_posts.text: Fireball 880, Adv+ 640, Skinny 40, Ultra 900. [Manawa Boogie Foiling reel]
Catching waves with lower motor pod in efoil position on axis integrated mast using fusion. Still can pump and stay on the bumps. Small 1-2ft shore break type of day...
riders_group_posts.notable: Testing efoil integrated mast for wave riding - motor in lower position. With Austin Keen.

key_comments.text: Can you feel a lot of drag though? I've never even considered trying to ride motor off? You think a Stoke Boost would be similar?

riders_group_posts.text: Hybrid Carbon foil boards. Was wondering if anyone in the know if the green sea foam color Axis hybrid carbon foil boards were designed by Frank that did the darts or another shaper? Anyone using them for parawing.
riders_group_posts.notable: Adrian Roper clarified: Pre-Frank involvement, Adrian designed the Hybrid and Blasts. Mark Shinn confirmed Hybrid 120L works for parawing but Mini Dart is better.

key_comments.text: Pre Frank's involvement. I designed the Hybrid and Blasts

Hi Doug, I have para-winged the Hybrid 120l in lightish winds. If you have one it works ok but not as well as a more dedicated board like the Mini Dart (which jumps out of the water with amazing ease). However if you are looking for one board to SUP fo... [see more]

riders_group_posts.text: Has anyone got experience using PNGv2 1200 for wake or wing foiling? I'd like to get one for dock starts but would like to know what it's like for other winging and wake foiling. Thanks! I'm a beginner at dock starts. Intermediate wing foiler and tow foiler. I use Art Pro v2 999 and HPS1050. I'm 77kg.

key_comments.text: I have used it for both. Wing in sub 10 knot wind. It's very slow and turns have to be deliberate and slow. Wake is fine. You can 15 wakes back but same thing. It likes to go straight and is super hard to turn.

Wide wings are a PITA to control on boat wakes, too wide to control well. Even the PNG 1150 was hard to ride on a boat wake. The couple times I've tried wider wings it wasn't worth it. Now to pull up in the flats and drop the handle to just pump is... [see more]

official_page_posts.text: We surveyed nearly 150 AXIS riders across 15+ countries to find out what they're riding, how they set it up, and what they'd change. Results: Top Disciplines: 1. Wing Foiling 2. Downwind SUP 3. Parawing 4. Prone. Who's Riding: Mostly 65-85 kg. 60% foiling 4+ years. Average rider does 3+ disciplines. Top Wing Models: Surge · Fireball · Spitfire. Top Regions: US West Coast · NZ/Australia · Europe. Quote: 'Turns off the back foot like surfing. The Surge is a game changer.'

key_comments.text: Thanks for this survey. I answered already and would be interested what percentage of answers is female riders (in each discipline if possible and especially dockstarting). Thanks!
Just pulled fresh from the survey (now at 241 responses): [data provided - female breakdown not visible in preview]
key_comments.note: AXIS responded to female rider question - shows engagement
